q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: etree.fromstring with parser configured as no_network=False and dtd_validation=True, enabling network I/O and external entity resolution

mock_guidance.target: Mock etree.fromstring to prevent actual XML parsing with the unsafe parser
mock_guidance.capture: Capture the raw XML string argument passed to etree.fromstring (str(xml)) to verify it contains user-controlled input from request.form['xml']
mock_guidance.fake_behavior: Return a harmless, fixed lxml ElementTree node (e.g., etree.Element('root')) so that downstream string concatenation with parsed_xml does not crash
notes: The dangerous parser is created on line 157 with no_network=False and dtd_validation=True; mocking etree.fromstring bypasses both the parser creation and the parse call.
Alternatively, patch etree.XMLParser to always inject resolve_entities=False and load_dtd=False, then let the real parse proceed on safe input.
